scheduler: Updated docs and comments
scheduler: Updated cache state machine diagram
scheduler: Changed upadteMetrics lock to RLock

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?

/kind cleanup
What this PR does / why we need it:
Assumed pods in the scheduler cache have a built-in expiration mechanism that sets a deadline for an assumed pod once binding is finished. This deadline is only set when TTL is configured, but currently it is hardcoded to zero so assumed pods never expire in cleanupAssumedPods.
Since there is no way to enable it with configuration, this code is effectively not in use and being removed in this PR.
Which issue(s) this PR is related to:

Fixed #106361

NONE

commit.message:
kubeadm: bump the CreatJob check timeout to 1 minute

During upgrade on Windows nodes the pause image is
bigger and there are various other factors that differ from
Linux.

Users can prepull images with `kubeadm config images pull
--kubernetes-version TARGET`, but it seems reasonable
to increase the rather short timeout of 15 seconds for
this preflight check.

Additionally users can always skip it with
--ignore-preflight-errors=...

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?
/kind feature

What this PR does / why we need it:
During upgrade on Windows nodes the pause image is bigger and there are various other factors that differ from Linux.
Users can prepull images with kubeadm config images pull --kubernetes-version TARGET, but it seems reasonable to increase the rather short timeout of 15 seconds for this preflight check.
Additionally users can always skip it with
--ignore-preflight-errors=...
Which issue(s) this PR is related to:

fixes kubernetes/kubeadm#3282
Special notes for your reviewer:
Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

kubeadm: bumped the timeout of the `kubeadm upgrade` `CreateJob` preflight check to 1 minute. This allows Windows worker nodes to have more time to run the preflight check. It uses the `pause` image, so if you are experiencing slow pull times, you can either pre-pull the new pause on the work using `kubeadm config images pull --kubernetes-version TARGET` or skip the preflight check with `--ignore-preflight-errors`.

@neolit123 there were 4 entries/versions before, not just 3, see 51fc3c7
Would it make sense to add one more release with 1.33 mapped, ideally to etcd 3.5.26 https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/blob/main/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-3.5.md#v3526-2025-12-17 to get etcd-io/etcd#20995 included in a 1.33 release, to avoid any issues upgrading k8s from 1.33 to 1.34 i.e. etcd from 3.5 to 3.6?

ideally, there should be 2 releases because kubeadm supports k8s versions N and N-1.
the reason we need 3 is because there is a period of time before a k8s release where this is needed. exactly after the release-x branch is created.
4 are not needed and that was some historical artifact.
if the etcd versions are somehow wrong across releases let's PR fixes, but keep the versions in the map capped to 3 in nearer releases.
See related https://etcd.io/docs/v3.6/upgrades/upgrade_3_6/

if you are upgrading from 1.33 to 1.34, you will get a 3.5.24 -> 3.6.5 upgrade:
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/release-1.33/cmd/kubeadm/app/constants/constants.go#L501
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/release-1.34/cmd/kubeadm/app/constants/constants.go#L504
then if you upgrade to 1.35 you will get 3.6.6:
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/release-1.35/cmd/kubeadm/app/constants/constants.go#L508

if you mean adding the 3.5.26 version instead of 3.5.24 version in the older releases, that's a decision up to SIG etcd.
you can ask about it in k8s slack #sig-etcd

commit.message:
kubeadm: revert to using a static list of etcd versions

The introduction of dynamic keys in the etcd version
map in constants.go introduced a couple of problems:

1. The size of the map could no longer be unit tested
because at UT runtime there was only one key "0".

2. Once a new k8s release branch is cut the version map
has mismatched versions. The latest k8s version mapped to the
future prerelease alpha (placeholder), the previous was the current
WIP release version and the oldest version in the map is the
current stable. This introduces a undesider shift of versions
where we are applying the wrong version to the current WIP
release unless an contrubutor PRs it.

The old static approach on the other hand is safer because
it hardcodes the versions, and the utility function
EtcdSupportedVersion() ensures that we get a relevant etcd
version even if the input k8s MINOR key is out of bonds for the map.

- Revert to using static version in the map.
- Revert the unit test TestEtcdSupportedVersionLength.
- Add additional comments over the map.

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?
/kind bug cleanup

What this PR does / why we need it:
The introduction of dynamic keys in the etcd version map in constants.go introduced a couple of problems
(#134866):


The size of the map could no longer be unit tested because at UT runtime there was only one key "0".


Once a new k8s release branch is cut the version map has mismatched versions. The latest k8s version mapped to the future prerelease alpha (placeholder), the previous was the current WIP release version and the oldest version in the map is the current stable. This introduces undesired shift of versions where we are applying the wrong version to the current WIP release unless a contributor PRs it.


The old static approach on the other hand is safer because it hardcodes the versions, and the utility function EtcdSupportedVersion() ensures that we get a relevant etcd version even if the input k8s MINOR key is out of bonds for the map.

Revert to using static version in the map.
Revert the unit test TestEtcdSupportedVersionLength.
Add additional comments over the map.

Which issue(s) this PR is related to:

NONE

slack discussion:

commit.message:
Skip memory pressure PSI test for CRI-O

Skip the memory pressure PSI test when running with CRI-O until automatic
memory.high configuration is available in the runtime. The test fails on
Fedora CoreOS due to different page cache reclaim behavior, and CRI-O is
implementing a fix to automatically set memory.high to 95% of memory.max
for cgroup v2 containers.

Signed-off-by: Sascha Grunert <sgrunert@redhat.com>

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?
/kind failing-test
What this PR does / why we need it:
Skip the memory pressure PSI test when running with CRI-O (used on Fedora CoreOS in test CI) until automatic memory.high configuration is available in the runtime.
The test fails on Fedora CoreOS due to different page cache reclaim behavior between kernels. In cgroup v2, memory.max is a hard limit that triggers OOM kills, while memory.high is a soft limit that triggers aggressive reclaim. Without memory.high configured, Fedora CoreOS doesn't reclaim page cache fast enough, causing instant OOM instead of sustained memory pressure.
CRI-O is implementing a fix to automatically set memory.high to 95% of memory.max for cgroup v2 containers, which will resolve this issue. Once that's available, this skip can be removed.
Which issue(s) this PR is related to:
Fixes #134141
Special notes for your reviewer:
Testgrid: https://testgrid.k8s.io/sig-node-cri-o#node-kubelet-serial-crio
Related:

CRI-O fix: cri-o/cri-o#9714
CoreOS tracker: coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker#2094

commit.message: fix(kubelet):resolve loop variable capture bug in WaitForAllPodsUnmount

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?
﻿
/kind bug
/kind flake
﻿
What this PR does / why we need it:
﻿
This PR fixes a goroutine closure bug in WaitForAllPodsUnmount where the loop variable pod was being captured by reference instead of by value.
﻿
Problem:
All goroutines created in the loop were capturing the same pod variable reference. When executed concurrently, they would all operate on the last pod in the slice instead of their intended pod. This caused intermittent test failures because some pods' volumes were never properly unmounted.
﻿
Solution:
Create a local copy of the loop variable within each iteration using pod := pod. This ensures each closure captures a distinct pod instance, allowing all pods to be processed correctly in parallel.
﻿
Which issue(s) this PR is related to:
﻿
Fixes #
#136255
﻿
﻿
Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
﻿
NONE

commit.message: Add DV support for cross field validators
validation-gen: Implement declarative validation native logic

Fix staticcheck lint error in validation-gen

Remove unnecessary assignment to blank identifier.
Remove non existent tags

pull_requests.bodyText:
What type of PR is this?
/kind feature
What this PR does / why we need it:
This pull request implements the Declarative Validation (DV) Native strategy. For new APIs and validations, the current framework often requires developers to maintain a parallel, handwritten Go implementation. This PR adopts a native validation strategy using the +k8s:declarativeValidationNative marker tag. When present on a field, the validation-gen tool ensures only stable validation rules are used and marks generated errors with .MarkDeclarativeNative(), allowing the system to identify and enforce these rules without a handwritten fallback.
Which issue(s) this PR is related to:

KEP-5073: Declarative Validation of Kubernetes Native Types With validation-gen

Special notes for your reviewer:

Error Struct Update: The Error struct in k8s.io/apimachinery now includes a DeclarativeNative boolean field.
The framework is also enforcing the tags used with the native tags are stable.

Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
NONE

Dependency updates like this are borderline for backports ... we sort of got lucky here that were no transitive updates we didn't want, and that the updates in hnslib were so constrained.
The feature-bounding approach hnslib has looks like it is prone to time-bomb feature disablement ... shouldn't the default for all features be support into the unbounded future? Are you adding tests to hnslib to make sure there's no repeat of this bug?
/lgtm

The feature-bounding approach hnslib has looks like it is prone to time-bomb feature disablement ... shouldn't the default for all features be support into the unbounded future? Are you adding tests to hnslib to make sure there's no repeat of this bug?

Thank you for approving my PR. The concerns you raised are completely valid. However, given the scenario where this feature is unsupported in the VFP stack but supported in the Cilium stack, along with the uncertainty around Windows binary release timelines, we had to take this approach. This is the only feature that currently does not have unbounded future support, and we will ensure that similar situations are avoided going forward.
/cc @dims

commit.message:
test/e2e: increase memory limits in pod resize tests

The pod resize e2e tests use memory limits as low as 20Mi for Guaranteed
QoS pods. On OpenShift/CRI-O, the container runtime (runc) runs inside
the pod's cgroup and requires ~20-22MB of memory during container
creation and restart operations. This causes intermittent OOM kills
when the pod's memory limit is at or below runc's memory footprint.

This issue does not occur on containerd-based clusters because
containerd's shim runs outside the pod's cgroup by default (ShimCgroup=""),
so runc's memory is not charged against the pod's limit.

Increase memory limits to provide sufficient headroom for runc:
- originalMem: 20Mi -> 35Mi
- reducedMem: 15Mi -> 30Mi
- increasedMem: 25Mi -> 40Mi

The test validates resize behavior, not minimal memory limits, so
larger values do not reduce test coverage.

Signed-off-by: Damien Grisonnet <dgrisonn@redhat.com>
